actionEn: Red — stop, yellow — prepare, do not move, green — go; do not enter the intersection on yellow.
descriptionEn: Regulates traffic at the intersection: green — you may proceed, yellow and red — movement prohibited.

nameEn: Traffic light with green arrow on red background
actionEn: A green arrow on a red background allows travel only in the direction it indicates — stop first, then proceed carefully toward the arrow.
descriptionEn: When a red signal and green arrow are lit together, after stopping at the light you may turn in the direction the arrow shows.

actionEn: You may only travel in the direction of the lit arrow — other directions have a red signal at that time.
descriptionEn: Applies only to drivers travelling in the direction shown by the lit arrow.

actionEn: The traffic light applies to each lane separately — follow the signal directly above your lane.
descriptionEn: Green signal — you may enter the lane where the light is located, red — entry prohibited.

actionEn: For pedestrians: red figure — stop, green — walk; if green is flashing, do not start crossing.
descriptionEn: Green signal — you may step onto the pedestrian crossing. A flashing green means the red signal will appear soon.

actionEn: A separate traffic light for cyclists — stop on a red bicycle symbol, go on green.
descriptionEn: Green signal — cyclists may enter the crossing. A flashing green means the red signal will appear soon.

actionEn: This light permits exiting a special lane (bus/tram) — follow the signal when exiting.
descriptionEn: A steady or flashing signal permits leaving the lane where the light is located and merging toward the side shown by the arrow.
